info.title: Alien Manager API
info.description: Control plane for Alien applications. Manages deployments, releases, commands, and telemetry.

get.summary: Get command payload (params and response) from KV
get.description:
Returns the raw params and response data stored in the manager's KV store.
Returns 404 if neither params nor response exist for this command.

put.summary: Store command payload data (params and/or response) directly into KV.
put.description:
Bypasses the command registry — useful for populating demo data or
migrating payload data. Does not validate command existence or state.

post.summary:
Every handler in this file runs `auth::require_auth(&state, &headers)`
and then threads `&subject` into the `DeploymentStore` calls — see the
trait doc on [`DeploymentStore`] for the convention.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by exact deployment name. Requires deploymentGroupId unless the token is scoped to a deployment group.

post.summary:
Every handler in this file runs `auth::require_auth(&state, &headers)`
and then threads `&subject` into the `DeploymentStore` calls. Embedders
that proxy to an upstream API can use the subject's `bearer_token` for
passthrough; single-tenant impls ignore it. See the trait doc on
[`DeploymentStore`] for the full convention.
post.description:
`POST /v1/deployments` — Inbound: workspace / project / dg bearer (or
authenticated user). Deployment-scoped tokens cannot create deployments.

post.summary:
`POST /v1/initialize` — Inbound: deployment-group bearer (typical),
or workspace bearer for self-hosted operator workflows. New deployments
are created via `DeploymentStore::create_deployment(caller, …)` so
embedders that proxy to an upstream API write the row in the dg's
workspace, not the manager's.

`POST /v1/rejoin` — re-acquire a deployment-scoped sync token for an
existing deployment by name. The OSS handler is intentionally lean:
the dg-token caller specifies the name, the store looks up the row,
and a fresh `Deployment` token is minted and returned. Returns
`404 Deployment not found` when no row matches — agents should fall
back to `/v1/initialize` in that case.
post.description:
Distinct from `/v1/initialize`'s idempotency branch because the agent
wants an explicit, distinguishable code path for "I lost local state,
please re-attach me" vs "I'm a brand-new pod claiming a name". The
platform-mode override forwards this to the SaaS rejoin endpoint
where audit / event semantics differ.

200.description: Existing deployment rejoined; fresh token returned

404.description: No deployment with that name in caller's deployment group

get.summary:
`GET /v1/releases` — Inbound: workspace / project bearer (or authenticated
user). Outbound: caller bearer (passthrough). Returns only releases the
caller may read.

post.description:
The body's `deploymentGroupToken` field is informational (mirrored back
for log correlation) — actual authentication is the standard `Authorization
Bearer` header processed by [`auth::require_auth`]. The handler tolerates
the body field being either the raw token or empty; it never reads
credentials from the body to make the secret path uniform with every
other endpoint.

404.description: Deployment group or release not found

post.summary:
`POST /v1/sync` — Inbound: deployment bearer. The agent-driven sync
path; `caller: &Subject` is threaded into the store so embedders see
the agent's own scope.

200.description: Agent sync response with optional target state

post.summary:
`POST /v1/sync/acquire` — Inbound: workspace / dg / deployment bearer.
`caller: &Subject` is threaded into `DeploymentStore::acquire` so
embedders can authorize against the inbound caller's scope.

`POST /v1/sync/reconcile` — Inbound: workspace / dg / deployment
bearer. `caller: &Subject` is threaded into `DeploymentStore::reconcile`.

`POST /v1/sync/release` — Inbound: workspace / dg / deployment bearer.
`caller: &Subject` is threaded into `DeploymentStore::release`.

200.description: Returns the identity of the authenticated caller

currentState.description:
Current deployment state as reported by the agent.
When present, the manager updates the deployment record to reflect
the agent's progress (status, stack_state, etc.).

operatorImageRepository.description:
Image repository the operator was pulled from (no tag), chart-injected.
Surfaced in the dashboard pin-version UI. Optional and Kubernetes-only.

operatorUpdate.description:
Outcome of the operator's in-flight self-update (structured
`OperatorUpdateReport`), forwarded verbatim to the platform reconcile so
the dashboard can show a truthful failed/in-progress state; opaque to the
standalone manager. Optional for back-compat.

resourceHeartbeats.description: Managed resource status samples emitted by pull-mode deployment steps.

commandsUrl.description:
Public URL for the commands API. Cloud-deployed workers use this
to poll for pending commands instead of the agent's local sync URL.

currentState.description:
Authoritative deployment state from the manager.

Returned when a pull deployment attaches with an empty local state while
the manager already has imported or previously reconciled state.

operatorTarget.description:
Desired agent self-update target. The payload carries either `binary`
(OS-service flow) or `helm` (Kubernetes flow); the agent picks the
one matching its regime.

AlienError.description:
Canonical error container that provides a structured way to represent errors
with rich metadata including error codes, human-readable messages, context,
and chaining capabilities for error propagation.

This struct is designed to be both machine-readable and user-friendly,
supporting serialization for API responses and detailed error reporting
in distributed systems.

code.description:
A unique identifier for the type of error.

This should be a short, machine-readable string that can be used
by clients to programmatically handle different error types.
Examples: "NOT_FOUND", "VALIDATION_ERROR", "TIMEOUT"

context.description:
Additional diagnostic information about the error context.

This optional field can contain structured data providing more details
about the error, such as validation errors, request parameters that
caused the issue, or other relevant context information.

httpStatusCode.description:
HTTP status code for this error.

Used when converting the error to an HTTP response. If None, falls back to
the error type's default status code or 500.

internal.description:
Indicates if this is an internal error that should not be exposed to users.

When `true`, this error contains sensitive information or implementation
details that should not be shown to end-users. Such errors should be
logged for debugging but replaced with generic error messages in responses.

message.description:
Human-readable error message.

This message should be clear and actionable for developers or end-users,
providing context about what went wrong and potentially how to fix it.

retryable.description:
Indicates whether the operation that caused the error should be retried.

When `true`, the error is transient and the operation might succeed
if attempted again. When `false`, retrying the same operation is
unlikely to succeed without changes.

source.description:
The underlying error that caused this error, creating an error chain.

This allows for proper error propagation and debugging by maintaining
the full context of how an error occurred through multiple layers
of an application.

neverPauses.description:
True when a `minCapacity: 0` instance has not reached 0 ACU over the observation
window — it is silently paying always-on prices (auto-pause verification).

AwsManagementConfig.description: AWS management configuration extracted from stack settings

managingRoleArn.description: The managing AWS IAM role ARN that can assume cross-account roles

AzureManagementConfig.description: Azure management configuration extracted from stack settings

managingTenantId.description: The managing Azure Tenant ID for cross-tenant access

oidcIssuer.description: OIDC issuer URL trusted by the target-side managed identity.

oidcSubject.description: OIDC subject claim trusted by the target-side managed identity.

BaseResource.description: Resource that can hold any resource type in the Alien system. All resources share common 'type' and 'id' fields with additional type-specific properties.

id.description: The unique identifier for this specific resource instance. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]). Maximum 64 characters.

BaseResourceOutputs.description: Resource outputs that can hold output data for any resource type in the Alien system. All resource outputs share a common 'type' field with additional type-specific output properties.

size.description: Size of the body in bytes

storageGetRequest.description: Pre-signed request for retrieving the body (for deployments)

storagePutUsed.description: Indicates storage upload was used for response submission

BodySpec.description: Body specification supporting inline and storage modes

CommandPayloadResponse.description: Payload response containing params and response data from KV

CommandState.description: Command states in the Commands protocol lifecycle

machine.description: Provider machine type selected for this deployment.
Provider machine type selected for this deployment.

ComputePoolSelection.description: User-selected deployment settings for one compute pool.

ComputeSettings.description:
Deployment-time compute choices for Alien-managed compute pools.

Application source declares portable pool requirements. This settings
object stores the concrete choices made for one deployment, such as the
provider machine type and selected machine counts.

pools.description: Selected compute choices keyed by pool ID.

CreateCommandRequest.description: Request to create a new command

storageUpload.description: Storage upload info (only for storage mode)

projectId.description:
Project this release belongs to. Required. The standalone server
uses the canonical value `"default"`.

The OSS CLI sends this field as `project` on `alien release`
(see `alien-cli` release flow); the underlying alien-managerx
release endpoint accepts both forms. Accept both here too so
`alien release` against an OSS standalone manager doesn't fail
at the schema layer with a confusing
`unknown field "project", expected "projectId"`.

CustomCertificateConfig.description: Platform-specific certificate references for custom domains.

kubernetes.description: Kubernetes TLS Secret reference for Secret-backed route profiles.

CustomDomainConfig.description: Custom domain configuration for a single resource.

domain.description: Fully qualified domain name to use.

projectId.description:
Required by the platform-SDK DeploymentGroup schema. Standalone
OSS mode is single-tenant, so we synthesize a project id
matching the workspace id.

workspaceId.description: Required by the platform-SDK DeploymentGroup schema.

DeploymentModel.description: Deployment model: how updates are delivered to the remote environment.

deploymentProtocolVersion.description:
Required by the platform-SDK Deployment schema. Hard-coded to
alien-core's `CURRENT_DEPLOYMENT_PROTOCOL_VERSION` so the CLI
accepts the response.

projectId.description:
Required by the platform-SDK Deployment schema. Standalone is
single-tenant; reuse the same synthetic project id used in the
deployment-groups route.

workspaceId.description: Required by the platform-SDK Deployment schema.

DomainSettings.description:
Domain configuration for the stack.

When `custom_domains` is set, the specified resources use customer-provided
domains and certificates. Otherwise, Alien auto-generates domains.

customDomains.description: Custom domain configuration per resource ID.

targetResources.description: Target resource patterns (null = all resources, Some = wildcard patterns)

value.description: Variable value (decrypted - deployment has access to decryption keys)

ErrorResponse.description: Error response wrapper for API endpoints

GcpManagementConfig.description: GCP management configuration extracted from stack settings

serviceAccountEmail.description: Service account email for management roles

HeartbeatsMode.description: How heartbeat health checks are handled.

ImportSourceKind.description:
Package source that produced an import request. Observability label
only — the manager does not branch on this value, and any new deployment
pathway can omit it without affecting import behavior.

id.description: Resource id from the active stack.

importData.description: Resolved typed payload for this resource.

type.description: Resource type from the active stack.

basePlatform.description:
Optional base cloud platform for Kubernetes setup targets such as
EKS/GKE/AKS. The runtime platform remains Kubernetes.

inputValues.description:
Deployer-provided stack inputs. Embedded platform managers resolve
these before creating the deployment; standalone managers accept the
field so generated setup clients have one stable initialize contract.

setupMethod.description:
Setup method that is registering this deployment, such as `manual` for
rendered Operator manifests or `helm` for generated Helm installs.

oneOf.description: Platform-managed cert imported into AWS ACM by the runtime.

region.description: ACM region. Defaults to the deployment region when omitted.

tags.description: Tags applied to runtime-imported ACM certificates.

oneOf.description: Platform-managed cert written to a Kubernetes TLS Secret.

secretNameTemplate.description: Secret name template. Runtime may substitute resource/deployment tokens.

oneOf.description: No TLS certificate should be configured by Alien.

KubernetesCertificateMode.description: Certificate publication or reference mode for Kubernetes public endpoints.

KubernetesCloudReference.description: Optional provider-specific identity for a cloud-backed Kubernetes cluster.

KubernetesClusterOwnership.description: Ownership model for the Kubernetes cluster.

cloud.description: Optional provider-specific cloud identity for existing clusters.

namespace.description: Namespace where the Alien chart and application resources run.

ownership.description:
Whether Alien should create the cluster, use a setup-owned existing
cluster, or bind to an external/on-prem cluster.

oneOf.description: Do not create Alien-managed external routing.
Use Alien-generated DNS and Platform-managed certificate material.

certificate.description: How managed certificate material reaches the route profile.

oneOf.description: Use a customer hostname and customer-owned certificate reference.

certificate.description: Customer-owned certificate reference consumed by the route profile.

domain.description: Hostname routed by the Kubernetes public endpoint.

KubernetesGatewayRouteProfile.description: Shared Gateway API route profile values.

controller.description: Route controller identifier, for example a cloud Gateway controller.

provider.description: Provider-specific route options that are required by the selected class.
Provider-specific route options that are required by the selected class.

KubernetesRouteProfile.description: Kubernetes route API selected for public endpoints.

oneOf.description: AWS ALB route options for EKS.

ipAddressType.description: Optional ALB IP address type, such as `dualstack`.

subnetIds.description: Explicit subnet IDs when the profile cannot rely on controller discovery.

staticAddressName.description: Optional static address name for the Gateway frontend.

oneOf.description: Azure Application Gateway for Containers route options.

albName.description: Optional ALB name when using BYO Application Gateway resources.

albNamespace.description: Optional ALB namespace when using BYO Application Gateway resources.

KubernetesRouteProviderOptions.description: Provider-specific route options required by supported managed profiles.

KubernetesSettings.description:
Kubernetes runtime substrate configuration.

This controls how setup chooses the cluster backing `Platform::Kubernetes`
deployments. When omitted, cloud-backed Kubernetes deployments default to a
managed cluster and generic/on-prem Kubernetes defaults to an external
cluster.

exposure.description: Public HTTPS exposure contract shared by setup, Helm, and runtime.

namespace.description: Secret namespace. Defaults to the release namespace when omitted.

maxLeases.description: Maximum number of leases to acquire

leases.description: Acquired leases (empty array if none available)

items.description: Releases the caller may read, newest first.

ManagementConfig.description:
Management configuration for different cloud platforms.

Platform-derived configuration for cross-account/cross-tenant access.
This is NOT user-specified - it's derived from the Manager's ServiceAccount.

oneOf.description:
Use the cloud provider's default VPC/network.

Designed for fast dev/test provisioning. No isolated VPC is created, so there
is nothing to wait for or clean up. VMs receive ephemeral public IPs for internet
access — no NAT gateway is provisioned.

- **AWS**: Discovers the account's default VPC. Subnets are public with auto-assigned IPs.
- **GCP**: Discovers the project's `default` network and regional subnet. Instance
  templates include an `AccessConfig` to assign an ephemeral external IP.
- **Azure**: Azure has no default VNet, so one is created along with a NAT Gateway.
  VMs stay private and use NAT for egress.

Not recommended for production. Use `Create` instead.

Create a new isolated VPC/VNet with a managed NAT gateway.

All networking infrastructure is provisioned by Alien and cleaned up on delete.
VMs use private IPs only; all outbound traffic routes through the NAT gateway.

Recommended for production deployments.

cidr.description:
VPC/VNet CIDR block. If not specified, auto-generated from stack ID
to reduce conflicts (e.g., "10.{hash}.0.0/16").

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VPC (AWS).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (NAT, proxy, VPN, etc.).

public_subnet_ids.description: IDs of public subnets (required for public ingress)

security_group_ids.description: Optional security group IDs to use

vpc_id.description: The ID of the existing VPC

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VPC (GCP).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (Cloud NAT, proxy, VPN, etc.).

network_name.description: The name of the existing VPC network

subnet_name.description: The name of the subnet to use

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VNet (Azure).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (NAT Gateway, proxy, VPN, etc.).

application_gateway_subnet_name.description: Name of the dedicated classic Application Gateway subnet within the VNet.

private_endpoint_subnet_name.description:
Name of the dedicated subnet that hosts Private Endpoints (e.g. for a
Postgres Flexible Server). A Private Endpoint must not share the private
subnet, which is already claimed by the Container Apps environment's
`infrastructure_subnet_id`. Required only when the stack contains a
Postgres resource; otherwise unused.

private_subnet_name.description: Name of the private subnet within the VNet

public_subnet_name.description: Name of the public subnet within the VNet

vnet_resource_id.description: The full resource ID of the existing VNet

NetworkSettings.description:
Network configuration for the stack.

Controls how VPC/VNet networking is provisioned. Users configure this in
`StackSettings`; the Network resource itself is auto-generated by preflights.

## Egress policy

Container cluster VMs are configured for egress based on the mode:

- `UseDefault` → VMs get ephemeral public IPs (no NAT is provisioned)
- `Create` → VMs use private IPs; Alien provisions a NAT gateway for outbound access
- `ByoVpc*` / `ByoVnet*` → no public IPs assigned; customer manages egress

For production workloads, use `Create`. For fast dev/test iteration, `UseDefault` is
sufficient. For environments with existing VPCs, use the appropriate `ByoVpc*` variant.

backend.description: Backend whose observer produced this snapshot.

complete.description:
Whether this batch is a complete replacement for the scope. Complete
batches tombstone previously observed rows in the same scope when they
are absent from `resources`.

controllerPlatform.description: Platform whose observer produced this snapshot.

inventoryScope.description: Stable scope for the provider list operation that produced this batch.

observedAt.description: Time the inventory scope was observed.

sourceKind.description:
Writer/source for this inventory pass, such as `operator` or
`manager-observer`.

providerKind.description:
Provider-native kind, such as `apps/v1/Deployment`,
`AWS::S3::Bucket`, `storage.googleapis.com/Bucket`, or an Azure
resource type.

rawIdentity.description:
Provider-native stable identity: Kubernetes object identity, cloud ARN,
GCP full resource name, Azure resource id, etc.

version.description: Release/version identity observed from the provider resource, when available.

detail.description: Optional human-readable detail from the Operator.

key.description: Stable capability key, such as `k8s-workloads` or `logs`.

state.description: Whether the capability is currently usable.

OperatorCapabilityState.description: State of an Operator capability as observed inside the environment.

PresignedOperation.description: The type of operation a presigned request performs

PresignedRequest.description:
A presigned request that can be serialized, stored, and executed later.
Hides implementation details for different storage backends.

backend.description: The storage backend this request targets

path.description: The path this request operates on

oneOf.description: HTTP-based request (AWS S3, GCP GCS, Azure Blob)

PresignedRequestBackend.description: Storage backend representation for different presigned request types

nativeImageHost.description:
Native image registry host for Lambda/Cloud Run.
Returned so push clients can set it on their local DeploymentConfig.

RejoinRequest.description:
`POST /v1/rejoin` — re-acquire a deployment-scoped sync token for an
agent whose persistent state was wiped (e.g. emptyDir on pod restart).

The agent calls this when `/v1/initialize` returns a name-conflict
error: the deployment row already exists, so creating it would 409,
but the agent legitimately needs a new sync token to keep operating
against it. Auth is the same dg bearer the chart originally mounted.

`name` is required — without it the server can't disambiguate which
row to reattach to.

setupFingerprints.description:
Setup-step fingerprints — used by `alien release` to short-circuit
re-pushing artifacts that haven't changed across releases. The
platform-API client requires this field to be present (even if
empty). The OSS standalone manager doesn't track per-setup-step
fingerprints, so we return an empty map.

resourceId.description:
Alien resource id, such as the `alien.Container` or `alien.Storage`
resource id from the stack.

ResourceLifecycle.description: Describes the lifecycle of a resource within a stack, determining how it's managed and deployed.

ResourceRef.description:
New ResourceRef that works with any resource type.
This can eventually replace the enum-based ResourceRef for full extensibility.

ResourceStatus.description: Represents the high-level status of a resource during its lifecycle.

ResourceType.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

maxInlineBytes.description: Maximum response body size that can be submitted inline

storageUploadRequest.description: Pre-signed request for uploading large response bodies

StackByPlatform.description:
The release API accepts stacks keyed by platform.
Only one platform stack needs to be present.

StackImportRequest.description: Request body for manager-side stack import.

basePlatform.description:
Optional base cloud platform for Kubernetes setup targets such as
EKS/GKE/AKS. The runtime platform remains Kubernetes.

deploymentName.description:
User-chosen deployment name. Must be unique within the deployment
group; the manager returns 409 on collision rather than silently
resolving to an existing deployment. Each setup adapter picks
the natural source: CloudFormation defaults to the CFN stack name,
Helm to `{namespace}/{release}`, Terraform requires an explicit
`name` attribute on the `alien_deployment` resource.

inputValues.description:
Deployer-provided stack input values collected by generated setup
surfaces. Platform-backed managers resolve these into runtime
environment variables before deployment creation; standalone managers
accept the field for setup package compatibility.

managementConfig.description:
Platform-derived management configuration, when this setup creates a
cross-account/cross-tenant management identity.

region.description: Region or location reported by the setup artifact.

releaseId.description:
Optional release id that produced the setup package. When
omitted, the manager imports against the latest release.

resourcePrefix.description:
Stable physical-name prefix used by the setup package for generated
resources. Runtime controllers use it when addressing imported
resources.

resources.description: Imported resources with typed per-resource payloads.

setupFingerprint.description: Setup compatibility fingerprint embedded in the package.

setupFingerprintVersion.description: Setup fingerprint algorithm version embedded in the package.

setupImportFormatVersion.description: Wire-format version for the setup import payload.

setupMetadata.description:
Setup source metadata needed by the control plane to guide privileged
teardown. The manager treats this as opaque JSON.

setupTarget.description: Setup target this package was generated for.

sourceKind.description:
Optional source label for observability. Does not affect import
behavior — the manager dispatches the same `ImporterRegistry`
regardless of which setup package emitted the payload.

stackSettings.description: Resolved stack settings supplied by the setup artifact.

StackImportResponse.description: Response body returned after a stack import.

deploymentToken.description: Deployment bearer token for the imported deployment, when available.

stackSettings.description: Stack settings persisted for the deployment.

StackResourceState.description: Represents the state of a single resource within the stack for a specific platform.

_internal.description:
The platform-specific resource controller that manages this resource's lifecycle.
This is None when the resource status is Pending.
Stored as JSON to make the struct serializable and movable to alien-core.

controllerPlatform.description:
Platform whose controller owns this resource state. Defaults to the
containing stack platform when absent.

dependencies.description:
Complete list of dependencies for this resource, including infrastructure dependencies.
This preserves the full dependency information from the stack definition.

error.description: Stores the last error encountered during a failed step transition.

lastFailedState.description:
Stores the controller state that failed, used for manual retry operations.
This allows resuming from the exact point where the failure occurred.
Stored as JSON to make the struct serializable and movable to alien-core.

lifecycle.description:
The lifecycle of the resource (Frozen or Live).
Defaults to Live if not specified.

outputs.description: Outputs generated by the resource (e.g., ARN, URL, Bucket Name).

previousConfig.description:
The previous resource configuration during updates.
This is set when an update is initiated and cleared when the update completes or fails.

remoteBindingParams.description:
Binding parameters for remote access.
Only populated when the resource has `remote_access: true` in its ResourceEntry.
This is the JSON serialization of the binding configuration (e.g., StorageBinding, VaultBinding).
Populated by controllers during provisioning using get_binding_params().

retryAttempt.description: Tracks consecutive retry attempts for the current state transition.

status.description: High-level status derived from the internal state.

type.description: The high-level type of the resource (e.g., Worker::RESOURCE_TYPE, Storage::RESOURCE_TYPE).

StackSettings.description:
User-customizable deployment settings specified at deploy time.

These settings are provided by the customer via CloudFormation parameters,
Terraform attributes, CLI flags, or Helm values. They customize how the
deployment runs and what capabilities are enabled.

**Key distinction**: StackSettings is user-customizable, while ManagementConfig
is platform-derived (from the Manager's ServiceAccount).

compute.description:
Deployment-time compute selections for Alien-managed compute pools.

This is where provider machine names such as EC2 instance types, GCE
machine types, or Azure VM SKUs belong. Application source should
declare portable requirements instead.

deploymentModel.description:
Deployment model: push (Manager) or pull (Agent).
Default: Push.
- Push: Manager drives updates. For cloud platforms, requires cross-account
  credentials established during initial setup. For push-mode local
  deployments (currently `alien dev`), the manager has direct access —
  no bootstrap needed.
- Pull: Agent in the target environment drives updates via polling.
  Required for Kubernetes and remote local deployments.

externalBindings.description:
External bindings for pre-existing infrastructure.
Allows using existing resources (MinIO, Redis, shared Container Apps
Environment, etc.) instead of having Alien provision them.
Required for Kubernetes platform, optional for cloud platforms.

heartbeats.description:
How heartbeat health checks are handled.
- off: No heartbeat permissions
- on: Heartbeat enabled (default)

network.description:
Network configuration for the stack (VPC/VNet settings).
If `None`, an isolated VPC with NAT is auto-created when the stack has resources
that require networking (e.g., containers). Set explicitly to customize:
`UseDefault` for the provider's default network (fast, dev/test only),
`Create` for an isolated VPC with managed NAT (production), or `ByoVpc*`
to reference an existing customer-managed VPC.

telemetry.description:
How telemetry (logs, metrics, traces) is handled.
- off: No telemetry permissions
- auto: Telemetry flows automatically (default)
- approval-required: Telemetry waits for explicit approval

updates.description:
How updates are delivered.
- auto: Updates deploy automatically (default)
- approval-required: Updates wait for explicit approval

StackState.description: Represents the collective state of all resources in a stack, including platform and pending actions.

platform.description: The target platform for this stack state.

resourcePrefix.description: A prefix used for resource naming to ensure uniqueness across deployments.

resources.description: The state of individual resources, keyed by resource ID.

putRequest.description: Pre-signed request for uploading command params

StorePayloadRequest.description:
Request to store payload data directly in KV by command_id.

This bypasses the normal command lifecycle (create → dispatch → respond)
and writes params/response directly into KV. Used by the demo service
to populate payload data for commands created outside the command flow.

SubmitResponseRequest.description: Request to submit a command response (from deployment)

TelemetryMode.description: How telemetry (logs, metrics, traces) is handled.

UpdatesMode.description: How updates are delivered to the deployment.

UploadCompleteRequest.description: Request to mark upload as complete

workspaceName.description:
Required by the platform-SDK `ServiceAccountSubject` parser when
the CLI looks up a deployment-group token by workspace name.

tags.name: stack-import
tags.description: Setup artifact stack import (CFN, TF, Helm)
